summary: Gruber reviews the iPhone 12 Mini and iPhone 12 Pro Max, the two size extremes of Apple's 2020 lineup. The 12 Mini is praised as a no-compromise smaller iPhone with roughly 85% of the regular 12's battery life, clever display scaling that matches the iPhone 11 Pro's pixel dimensions, and a $100 lower price tag. The 12 Pro Max has the best camera system Apple has made, with a 47% larger sensor and 2.5× telephoto, but is simply too big for Gruber's taste. He uses a golf analogy from Harvey Penick to frame the regular iPhone 12 as the baseline from which all other models can be understood. The Mini emerges as the best value in the lineup.
key_points: The iPhone 12 Mini is functionally identical to the iPhone 12 but smaller, with no meaningful compromises beyond roughly 85% battery life

The 12 Mini's display uses clever 96% downsampling to match the iPhone 11 Pro's exact pixel dimensions (2436×1125), maintaining app compatibility while achieving 476 PPI — the highest pixel density Apple has shipped

The 12 Pro Max camera module is physically much larger than other iPhone 12 models, with lenses protruding 1.25mm further, and this size difference is why the better camera system can't simply be put in the smaller Pro

The 12 Pro Max's 2.5× telephoto (65mm equivalent) produces noticeably more flattering portraits than the 12 Pro's 2.0× (52mm), though the larger sensor's low-light advantages weren't dramatically evident in casual shooting
At $100 less than the iPhone 12, the Mini represents exceptional value — miniaturization that costs less rather than more
money_quotes: If you've been waiting for a smaller Face-ID-era iPhone and haven't yet preordered a 12 Mini — waiting, perhaps, for the reviews, to see if there's a catch — stop reading and place your order. There is no catch.
The 12 Mini is a triumph and a joy to use.
Walking around with the 12 Pro Max in one pocket and the 12 Mini in the other feels like I'm carrying a little iPhone for the big iPhone to use.
I don't care if the camera is so good it can see through walls, this size and weight iPhone is just not something I want to carry around.
The result feels like the 2020 iPhone — the one and only 2020 iPhone — that we would have had if iPhones had never gotten bigger or forked into Pro and non-Pro tiers.
The no-adjective iPhone 12 is the 7-iron of Apple's iPhone lineup.

That the iPhone 12 Mini costs $100 less than the iPhone 12 feels too good to be true. I suspect most of the people who want a 12 Mini don't want it because it costs less, they want it entirely because they prefer the size. It's like Apple is just handing these people a $100 bill along with the no-compromise smaller iPhone they've been waiting for.
key_insight: The iPhone 12 Mini is a rare case of miniaturization without meaningful compromise, delivering the same experience as larger iPhones at a lower price — making it the best value in Apple's lineup for those who prefer a smaller phone.
